title: Making dropdown conditions based on previous column selection
excerpt:

I currently have a board set up for Accounts and another board set up for Contacts. In the contacts board, there is a mirror column that allows you to select what company the new contact you create is associated …

posts.author: zperp (Zach Perpich)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello!

I currently have a board set up for Accounts and another board set up for Contacts. In the contacts board, there is a mirror column that allows you to select what company the new contact you create is associated to.

In our ordering board, I would like to create a contact dropdown where I can select a customer contact rather than having to type it in. This is easily done with a mirror column - But I can’t seem to figure out a way to set this up so when I am selecting the customer contact, it is only allowing me to select customers that are associated to the account I am ordering for. Is this possible?

Example:
-In Accounts Board I create Account Name “Example Inc”
-In the Contacts Board, I create Contact “John Doe” and select “Example Inc” in the Mirror Column to link “John Doe” to the “Example Inc” Account.
-I then am in the Ordering Board, entering an order for “Example Inc” and I am selecting who the customer contact will be for the order. I only want the options for this mirror column dropdown to be customer contacts associated with “Example Inc”

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Dependency between columns (both connect boards [your example], or status columns) is not possible - even with third party apps in the main board view. (It could be created in an app view by third party)
